serviceNotes.brake fluid: Brake fluid is hygroscopic and absorbs moisture over time, reducing its boiling point and braking effectiveness. Replacement is time-based, not distance-based — typically every 2 years regardless of mileage.
serviceNotes.check front brake pads: BMW CBS-style front axle pad inspection; strong regen on BEV often extends pad life—inspections still required.
serviceNotes.check rear brake pads: BMW CBS-style rear axle pad inspection; strong regen on BEV often extends pad life—inspections still required.
serviceNotes.coolant flush: High-voltage battery and e-drive cooling circuit: BMW publishes time/distance-based coolant service for EVs; 60,000 km is a planning proxy—interval estimated, confirm official service data.
serviceNotes.gearbox fluid: Reduction gear / drive unit fluid (BEV): officially often "lifetime"; 100,000 km mfr row is interval estimated for long-term wear metals in oil—specialist preventive change. "xDrive" on iX denotes electric all-wheel drive—there is no ICE xDrive transfer case or conventional differential oil service like petrol/diesel BMWs.
serviceNotes.micro filter: Microfilter replacement per BMW iX EU maintenance information; 20,000 km is a common planning interval—verify CBS and regional booklet.
